questions.text: Each problem consists of three statements. Based on the first two statements, the third statement may be true, false, or uncertain. Tanya is older than Eric. Cliff is older than Tanya. Eric is older than Cliff. If the first two statements are true, the third statement is:
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.explanation: Because the first two statements are true, Eric is the youngest of the three, so the third statement must be false.

questions.text: Directions: Read the following information to answer.Rizwan and Kashif are good in Hockey and Volleyball. Saeed and Rizwan are good in Hockey and Baseball. Ghafoor and Kashif are good in Cricket and Volleyball. Saeed, Ghafoor and Muneeb are good in Football and Baseball.Who is good in Baseball, Volleyball and Hockey?
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.explanation: Arranging the statement we get; Rizwan is good in Hockey, Volleyball and Baseball. Kashif is good in Hockey, Volleyball and Cricket Saeed is good in Hockey, Baseball and Football. Ghafoor is good in Cricket, Volleyball and Football. Muneeb is good in Football. So, correct option is C i.e., Rizwan is good in Hockey, Volleyball and Baseball.

questions.text: Each problem consists of three statements. Based on the first two statements, the third statement may be true, false, or uncertain. All the offices on the 9th floor have wall-to-wall carpeting. No wall-to-wall carpeting is pink. None of the offices on the 9th floor has pink wall-to-wall carpeting. If the first two statements are true, the third statement is:
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.explanation: If no wall-to-wall carpeting is pink and all the offices have wall-to-wall carpeting, none of the offices has pink wall-to-wall carpeting.

questions.text: Each problem consists of three statements. Based on the first two statements, the third statement may be true, false, or uncertain. Mara runs faster than Gail. Lily runs faster than Mara. Gail runs faster than Lily. If the first two statements are true, the third statement is:
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.explanation: We know from the first two statements that Lily runs fastest. Therefore, the third statement must be false.

questions.text: Each problem consists of three statements. Based on the first two statements, the third statement may be true, false, or uncertain. Blueberries cost more than strawberries. Blueberries cost less than raspberries. Raspberries cost more than both strawberries and blueberries. If the first two statements are true, the third statement is:
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.explanation: Because the first two statements are true, raspberries are the most expensive of the three.

questions.text: Statements and Conclusions: Statements: Papers are pens. Angle is a paper. Conclusions I. Angle is not a pen. II. Angle is a pen.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: From the given statement we know that Papers are pens and Angle is a paper so now we know from the first statement that paper is a pen as paper is the same as angle so only conclusion II follows.
This option is incorrect because neither conclusion I nor conclusion II follows from the given statements. We cannot choose either conclusion as both are unsupported.

questions.text: Each problem consists of three statements. Based on the first two statements, the third statement may be true, false, or uncertain. During the past year, Josh saw more movies than Stephen. Stephen saw fewer movies than Darren. Darren saw more movies than Josh. If the first two statements are true, the third statement is:
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.explanation: Because the first two sentences are true, both Josh and Darren saw more movies than Stephen. However, it is uncertain as to whether Darren saw more movies than Josh.

questions.text: Analogies:Marathon is to race as Hibernation is to
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: a) WinterExplanation: A marathon is a specific type of race that involves running a long distance, usually 26.2 miles (42.195 kilometers). The relationship between marathon and race is that a marathon is a specific type of race.

b) BearExplanation: This option does not establish a clear analogy between hibernation and bear. While it is true that some bears hibernate during the winter, hibernation is not exclusive to bears. Other animals also hibernate during the winter. The analogy should focus on the behavior of hibernation rather than a specific animal.

c) DreamExplanation: This option does not establish a clear analogy between hibernation and dream. Hibernation is a biological state of dormancy and inactivity that some animals experience during the winter to survive harsh conditions. Dreams are mental experiences that occur during sleep and are not directly related to hibernation.

d) SleepExplanation: This option establishes a clear analogy between hibernation and sleep. Hibernation is a specific behavior exhibited by some animals during the winter to conserve energy and survive harsh conditions. Sleep is a state of rest and reduced activity that all animals, including humans, experience regularly. The relationship between hibernation and sleep is that hibernation is a specific type of prolonged sleep exhibited by certain animals.

questions.explanation: 'Marathon' can be regarded as an extensive and extended form of 'race'. Similarly, 'hibernation' is an a extended form of 'sleep'.

questions.text: Verbal Classification:Which one of the following is a different one?
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Skin is organ inside the body. this option is incorrect.
Lungs is organ inside the body. this option is incorrect.
Heart is organ inside the body.this option is incorrect.
Life is the existence of an individual human being or animal hence out of the four it is different/the odd one out.

questions.explanation: Skin, Lungs, and Heart are all organs inside the body. Life is the existence of an individual human being or animal hence out of the four it is different/the odd one out.

questions.text: Statements and Conclusions:All fish are tortoises. No tortoise is a crocodile.Conclusions: I. No crocodile is a fishII. No fish is a crocodile
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: a) Only conclusion II followsConclusion II states, "No fish is a crocodile." This conclusion follows directly from the given statements. If all fish are tortoises (Statement 1) and no tortoise is a crocodile (Statement 2), then it logically follows that no fish can be a crocodile. So, conclusion II is correct.
b) Either I or II followsThis option is not correct. Both conclusions are not contradictory, and they can both be true simultaneously. So, it is not a case of either/or.
c) Neither I nor II followsThis option is not correct. Conclusion II ("No fish is a crocodile") follows from the given statements.

The first statement tells us that all fish are tortoises. This tells us that no crocodiles are fish hence conclusion one is correct. The second conclusion also reiterates the same thing thus both conclusions follow.

questions.explanation: The first statement tells us that all fish are tortoises. This tells us that no crocodiles are fish hence conclusion one is correct. The second conclusion also reiterates the same thing thus both conclusions follow.

questions.text: Statements: All fish are tortoises. No tortoise is a crocodile. Conclusions: I. No crocodile is a fish. II. No fish is a crocodile.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: STATEMENT 1, All fish = tortoise STATEMENT 2, Tortoise crocodile CONCLUSION 1, crocodile fish CONCLUSION 2, fish crocodile Both conclusions are the same and as Statement 1 says all fish are tortoise and Statement 2 perfectly denies the tortoise being a crocodile so both Conclusions FOLLOW.

questions.explanation: STATEMENT 1, All fish = tortoise STATEMENT 2, Tortoise crocodile CONCLUSION 1, crocodile fish CONCLUSION 2, fish crocodile Both conclusions are the same and as Statement 1 says all fish are tortoise and Statement 2 perfectly denies the tortoise being a crocodile so both Conclusions FOLLOW.

The statement discusses about eradicating corruption and prejudice from society, this indicates that these aspects are undesirable. So, I is not implicit. So, II is implicit and is the most appropriate answer for this question..

options.explanation: this option is incorrect.
this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: The statement talks of eradicating corruption and prejudice from society, which indicates that these aspects are undesirable. So, I is not implicit. Besides, the statement mentions reforming the administrative system. So, II is implicit.

questions.text: Statements and conclusions: All benches are chairs . Some chairs are roads . All roads are pillars. Conclusions I - Some pillars are benches II - Some pillars are chairs. Ill - Some roads are benches IV - No pillar is bench
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: Only either I or IV, and Ill follows
Only either I or IV follows
Only either I or IV, and II follows
options.explanation: I. Some pillars are benches. All benches are desks and Some desks are roads. All roads are pillars. So some pillars are desks, but may not be pillars II. Some pillars are desks. True, Some desks are roads. All roads are pillars. So some pillars are desks.III. Some roads are benches. False, some roads are desks and some desks are benches, but roads may not be benches.IV. No pillar is a bench. All benches are desks and Some desks are roads. All roads are pillars. So some pillars are desks, but may not be pillars.Thus only either I or IV and II follow

questions.explanation: I. Some pillars are benches. All benches are desks and Some desks are roads. All roads are pillars. So some pillars are desks, but may not be pillars II. Some pillars are desks. True, Some desks are roads. All roads are pillars. So some pillars are desks.III. Some roads are benches. False, some roads are desks and some desks are benches, but roads may not be benches.IV. No pillar is a bench. All benches are desks and Some desks are roads. All roads are pillars. So some pillars are desks, but may not be pillars.Thus only either I or IV and II follow

questions.text: (1) Mehwish is standing on a chair behind Abdullah (2) Abdullah is standing on the chair behind Bilal (3) Bilal is standing on the chair behind Mehwish. If the first two statements are correct, then the third one is:
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.explanation: If Mehwish is standing on a chair behind Abdullah and Abdullah is behind Bilal, that means Bilal is on the first chair. So, Bilal can not be behind Mehwish.

questions.text: During the past year, Jamshed saw more movies than Sadia.Sadia saw fewer movies than Dawood.Dawood saw more movies than Jamshed.If the first two statements are true, the third statement is
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Incorrect
Incorrect
Explanation given below
Incorrect

questions.explanation: Statement 1 = Jamshed > Sadia (True) Statement 2 = Sadia < Dawood or Dawood > Sadia (True) Statement 3 = Dawood > Jamshed (Uncertain) Since Statement 1 and 2 both imply that Sadia has seen less number of movies than, both Dawood and Jamshed, there is uncertainty about who has seen more movies between Jamshed and Dawood as not enough information is given. It can either be Dawood > Jamshed > Sadia OR Jamshed > Dawood > Sadia .
